gutachter_partner_marketing.hero_intro: Client acquisition for appraisers — offline, online and via platforms, properly combined.
gutachter_partner_marketing.antwort_capsule: Successful marketing for car appraisers rests on three pillars: local visibility (Google Business Profile, reviews, local SEO), a convincing online presence (website, references) and distribution via established platforms. While ads quickly burn budget, local reputation and platform connection build a predictable, low-cost order base.

aspekte.text: Repair-shop cooperations, referrals from satisfied customers and local networks are the traditional source of orders. Reliable, but limited in scalability and heavily dependent on your personal contact network.

A well-maintained Google Business Profile, genuine customer reviews and local SEO determine whether you are found for “car appraiser + city”. This is the biggest low-cost lever for regional appraisers.
A clear website with services, qualifications (DAT, BVSK, public appointment) and references builds trust. It is the landing page for every marketing measure — without it, reach fizzles out.
Via established referral platforms like Claimondo, you reach qualified claims without investing in ads yourself. Regional activation instead of cost-per-lead — predictable and low-risk.

faqs.frage: Which marketing brings car appraisers the most orders?
faqs.antwort: The combination of local visibility (Google Business Profile, reviews) and platform distribution. Both build a predictable order base without ongoing ad budget — unlike ads.

faqs.frage: Are Google Ads worth it for appraisers?
faqs.antwort: Ads bring quick visibility but are expensive and end as soon as the budget stops. For most regional appraisers, local SEO plus platform connection is more sustainable and cheaper.

faqs.frage: How important are online reviews?
faqs.antwort: Very important. Reviews are a central ranking and trust factor in local search. Actively ask satisfied customers for a review — it is free and acts directly on the order situation.

faqs.frage: Do I need my own website as an appraiser?
faqs.antwort: Yes — it is the trust landing page for every marketing measure. It should clearly show services, qualifications and references. Without it, the reach of other channels fizzles out.

faqs.frage: How does a platform complement my own marketing?
faqs.antwort: A platform handles acquisition and first contact for part of your workload, while your own marketing (local, referrals) runs in parallel. This lowers your acquisition risk without giving up your independence.

schritte.text: Maintain your Google Business Profile, collect reviews, one local page per catchment area. It works sustainably but takes months to reach top rankings — and you compete with platforms and repair shops for the same terms.

Ads on high-intent terms like “car appraiser [city]”. Delivers clicks immediately but is expensive: CPCs of €8–12, realistic enquiry costs of €80–250 — and the budget runs whether an assignment results or not.

Claimondo runs the marketing centrally (SEO, ads, landing pages) and distributes pre-qualified claimant enquiries to verified appraisers in the network — matched to your catchment area. No ad budget of your own, a predictable flow of assignments.

A qualified lead is a claim enquiry with a no-fault accident, clarified liability and damage above the de minimis threshold — i.e. a case with a full appraisal that the at-fault liability insurer covers (§ 249 BGB). These are exactly the cases distributed in the network.

gutachter_partner_leads.faq_h2: Frequently asked questions about lead generation for appraisers

faqs.frage: As a car appraiser, how do I win new assignments predictably?
faqs.antwort: Three levers: (1) local visibility (Google Business Profile, reviews, local landing pages per catchment area), (2) paid search on high-intent terms like “car appraiser [city]” — expensive, on average €8–12 CPC, (3) platform distribution: an intermediary delivers pre-qualified claimant enquiries without your own marketing budget. The mix decides — purely organic is slow, purely ads is expensive, platform leads are predictable.

faqs.frage: What does a qualified car appraiser lead cost?
faqs.antwort: Via paid search, the cost per enquiry is realistically €80–250, because generic terms are expensive and conversion rates are low. Via a platform with a flow of claimants, the upfront budget is eliminated — you take on a pre-qualified assignment from your catchment area. Claimondo distributes enquiries to verified appraisers in the network.

faqs.frage: What distinguishes a qualified lead from a mere address?
faqs.antwort: A qualified appraiser lead is a concrete claim enquiry from a claimant with a no-fault accident, a clarified question of liability and damage above the de minimis threshold — i.e. a case where a full appraisal arises and the at-fault liability insurer pays (§ 249 BGB). Plain address lists without a claim context barely convert.

faqs.frage: Do I need a marketing budget to join the Claimondo partner network?
faqs.antwort: No. The marketing (SEO, Google Ads, landing pages) runs centrally through Claimondo. As an appraiser you receive pre-qualified enquiries from your region and focus on the appraisal. The prerequisite is verification as an independent car appraiser.

gutachter_partner_leads.cta_p: Verified car appraisers receive pre-qualified enquiries from their catchment area — without their own ad budget.

kfz_gutachter_gutachten_service.hero_h1: Car Appraisal Service — independent, fast, free of charge
kfz_gutachter_gutachten_service.hero_intro: From the independent damage appraisal to the payout — one service that coordinates everything.
kfz_gutachter_gutachten_service.antwort_capsule: A car appraisal service produces a neutral damage appraisal and coordinates the entire settlement. The appraisal documents repair costs, market value reduction (diminished value), replacement and residual value to DAT/Audatex. In a no-fault accident, the at-fault liability insurer bears the cost (§ 249 BGB) — via a security assignment (§ 398 BGB), the expert settles directly with it, €0 for you. Claimondo combines the appraisal with scheduling in under 48 hours, repair-shop coordination and legal enforcement.

aspekte.text: A certified, independent expert documents the amount of damage, repair path, market value reduction as well as replacement and residual value — court-proof and neutral, not in the insurer’s interest.
We match you with the nearest available expert. The appointment takes place where you are — at home, at work or in the repair shop. If the vehicle is not roadworthy, we arrange towing.

aspekte.kicker: Repair shop, rental car, law firm
aspekte.text: The service connects appraisal, partner repair shop, rental car or loss-of-use, and the specialised traffic-law firm. You have one contact instead of five.

The law firm claims repair costs, diminished value, rental car, loss-of-use and, where applicable, compensation for pain and suffering directly from the at-fault insurer. If it reduces the amount, a follow-up claim or lawsuit follows — on success the other side bears the costs.

faqs.frage: What does a car appraisal include?
faqs.antwort: Repair cost calculation (DAT/Audatex), market value reduction, replacement value, residual value in case of total loss, photo documentation, repair path and course of damage. The appraisal is the basis for the entire claim settlement.

faqs.frage: What does the appraisal service cost?
faqs.antwort: In a no-fault accident with damage over around €750: €0. The at-fault liability insurer bears the fee (§ 249 BGB); the expert settles directly with it via a security assignment (§ 398 BGB). Only in case of own fault or a minor claim do you bear the costs yourself.

faqs.frage: How quickly do I get an appointment?
faqs.antwort: Usually within 48 hours. The expert comes to you — home, workplace or repair shop. The written report usually follows 1–2 days after the inspection.

faqs.frage: Do I also get the appraisal digitally?
faqs.antwort: Yes. You receive the full appraisal report digitally as a PDF — on request additionally by post. The partner law firm uses the appraisal directly for the claim against the at-fault insurer.

faqs.frage: Is an appraisal worth it for minor damage?
faqs.antwort: For visually minor damage over around €750, usually yes — hidden consequential damage (control units, side members) otherwise goes undetected. Below the de minimis threshold of ~€750, a repair shop’s cost estimate is usually sufficient.

kfz_gutachter_gutachten_service.cta_p: Reported in 5 minutes — we coordinate appraisal, repair shop and lawyer. In case of third-party fault, €0 for you.

kfz_gutachter_autoschaden_soforthilfe.hero_intro: The 5 most important immediate steps after an accident — handled correctly, you secure repair, diminished value and all further claims.
kfz_gutachter_autoschaden_soforthilfe.antwort_capsule: Right after car damage, five things matter: 1) secure the accident scene and help the injured, 2) preserve evidence (photos, data, witnesses) and call the police if fault is unclear, 3) make no admission of fault on the phone and sign nothing, 4) commission your own independent appraiser — not the at-fault insurer’s, 5) report the claim and bring in a lawyer in case of third-party fault. In a no-fault accident the other side bears all costs (§ 249 BGB) — €0 for you.

schritte.text: Hazard lights on, set up the warning triangle (in town ~50 m, out of town ~100 m, motorway ~150–400 m), put on a hi-vis vest. Help the injured and call 112 immediately for personal injury. Get yourself out of the danger zone.

Photos from several angles: damage, final position of both vehicles, number plates, skid marks, traffic signs. Note the other party’s details (name, address, insurer, plate, driver). Approach witnesses. If fault is unclear, hit-and-run or personal injury: call the police (110).

Make no admission of fault — neither at the scene nor on the phone — and sign no statement from the at-fault insurer. If the other side offers a “quick, free” handling, that is claim steering — politely refer them to your appraiser and lawyer.

For damage over around €750 you are entitled to your own independent expert (BGH VI ZR 67/06). Do not commission the at-fault insurer’s appraiser — they work in its interest. Ideally an appointment before any repair.

Report the claim — with Claimondo online in 5 minutes. We coordinate appraiser, repair shop and partner law firm. In case of third-party fault, the at-fault liability insurer also bears the lawyer’s costs (§ 249 BGB).

faqs.frage: Do I have to call the police after car damage?
faqs.antwort: It is mandatory for personal injury, hit-and-run or significant property damage with unclear fault. For clear minor dents with a consensual exchange of details, the police are not strictly necessary — but a police report later helps as evidence. When in doubt, call (110).

faqs.frage: What should I photograph?
faqs.antwort: Damage to both vehicles from several angles, the final position of the vehicles in the traffic area, all number plates, skid marks, debris, traffic signs and lights. Also the other party’s driving licence and insurance details. Better too many photos than too few.

faqs.frage: May I have my car repaired before the appraiser comes?
faqs.antwort: No — wait for the appraisal. If repairs happen before the assessment, the damage can no longer be documented neutrally, and the insurer reduces or refuses reimbursement. An appraiser appointment is usually possible within 48 hours.

faqs.frage: Do I have to give information to the at-fault insurer?
faqs.antwort: You are not obliged to give the at-fault insurer details about the course of events, fault or injuries on the phone. Such statements are later readily used against you. Politely refer them to your appraiser and your lawyer.

faqs.frage: What if the question of fault is unclear?
faqs.antwort: Then preserving evidence and the police are especially important. An accident sketch, witnesses and photos later decide the liability ratio. Even with partial fault, your own appraiser is worth it — your damage is reimbursed proportionally, and a neutral appraisal prevents the other side’s inflated contributory-fault ratios.

kfz_gutachter_autoschaden_soforthilfe.cta_p: Reported in 5 minutes — we coordinate appraiser, repair shop and lawyer. In case of third-party fault, €0 for you.

kfz_gutachter_sv_vs_gutachter.hero_h1: Expert or appraiser — what is the difference?
kfz_gutachter_sv_vs_gutachter.hero_intro: In a car claim, both terms mean the same — what matters is not the title but independence and qualification.
kfz_gutachter_sv_vs_gutachter.antwort_capsule: In everyday use, “Kfz-Sachverständiger” (expert) and “Kfz-Gutachter” (appraiser) are used synonymously — both produce a damage appraisal. A legally regulated difference exists only for the additional title “publicly appointed and sworn” (§ 36 GewO), which requires an IHK expertise examination. For settling a no-fault accident, what matters most is that the expert is independent and that you may choose them freely (BGH VI ZR 67/06) — not the at-fault insurer.

aspekte.text: “Appraiser” and “expert” are interchangeable in a car claim — both assess the amount of damage, diminished value and repair path. Neither term as such is legally protected.

Reputable car experts are certified to DIN EN ISO 17024 (DEKRA, KÜS, TÜV, DAT) or organised in the BVSK. The highest level is public appointment and swearing-in by the IHK (§ 36 GewO) — examined, special expertise.

The at-fault insurer’s appraiser works for the insurer — their goal is a low claim amount. Your own, independent expert assesses neutrally in your interest. In case of third-party fault, the other side bears the cost of your expert (§ 249 BGB).

You may choose your expert freely — the at-fault insurer may not impose one on you (“claim steering”). The cost of your own appraisal is a reimbursable item of damage, provided it is not a minor claim (under around €750).

faqs.frage: Is it Kfz-Sachverständiger or Kfz-Gutachter?
faqs.antwort: Both are correct — in a car claim the terms are used synonymously. Neither “expert” nor “appraiser” is a legally protected job title. Only the additional title “publicly appointed and sworn” (§ 36 GewO) is regulated and requires a passed IHK expertise examination.

faqs.frage: Do I need my own appraiser after an accident?
faqs.antwort: After a no-fault accident with damage over around €750, yes. Your own independent appraisal documents repair costs, diminished value and replacement value in your interest. The cost is borne by the at-fault liability insurer as part of the damage (§ 249 BGB). For minor claims under ~€750, a cost estimate is usually sufficient.

faqs.frage: What does “publicly appointed and sworn” mean?
faqs.antwort: These experts have passed an examination of special expertise before the Chamber of Industry and Commerce (IHK) (§ 36 GewO) and are sworn to impartiality. It is the highest qualification level and carries particular weight in court. Alongside this, certifications to DIN EN ISO 17024 (DEKRA, KÜS, TÜV, DAT) and BVSK membership are recognised quality features.

faqs.frage: May the at-fault insurer prescribe an appraiser for me?
faqs.antwort: No. You have the right to freely choose your expert (BGH VI ZR 67/06). If the at-fault insurer offers its “own” appraiser or a “free” claim handling, that is claim steering in its interest — you may decline and commission an independent expert.

faqs.frage: What should I look for when choosing?
faqs.antwort: Independence (no contract with the at-fault insurer), a recognised certification or public appointment, calculation-proof software (DAT/Audatex) and fast regional availability. Claimondo refers exclusively independent, qualified experts near you — an appointment usually within 48 hours.

kfz_gutachter_sv_vs_gutachter.cta_h2: Had an accident? Secure your own appraiser.
kfz_gutachter_sv_vs_gutachter.cta_p: Independent, qualified, near you — appointment usually within 48 hours. In case of third-party fault, €0 for you.
